question: How do I change my billing cycle from monthly to annual?
expected_answer: To change your billing cycle from monthly to annual: 1. Log in to the CloudScale Console. 2. Select your Organization. 3. Click on Billing & Subscriptions in the left sidebar. 4. Click Manage Plan on your active plan card. 5. Toggle to Annual Billing and click Confirm Changes. You will get a 20% discount, and any remaining days on monthly billing will be credited.
reference_context: To change your billing cycle from monthly to annual: 1. Log in to the CloudScale Console. 2. Select your Organization from the top-left dropdown. 3. Click on Billing & Subscriptions in the left sidebar navigation. 4. Locate your active plan card and click Manage Plan. 5. Select the Annual Billing toggle. 6. Click Confirm Changes. Your account will be charged the discounted annual rate immediately, and a prorated credit for any remaining days in your current monthly cycle will be applied to your invoice. Annual billing offers a 20% discount compared to monthly payments.

question: What is the pricing model for Managed Redis databases?
expected_answer: Managed Redis is priced hourly by RAM capacity and replication: Cache-T1 (1GB RAM, single node) is $0.015/hour (~$11/month), Cache-M1 (4GB RAM, HA replica) is $0.08/hour (~$58/month), and Cache-Production (16GB RAM, cluster mode, multi-replica) is $0.32/hour (~$230/month). Public egress is $0.08/GB, but local region data transfer is free.
reference_context: CloudScale Managed Redis databases are priced hourly based on the memory capacity and replication configuration: - Cache-T1 (1GB RAM, Single Node): $0.015/hour (~$11/month) - Cache-M1 (4GB RAM, HA with Replica): $0.08/hour (~$58/month) - Cache-Production (16GB RAM, Cluster Mode, Multi-Replica): $0.32/hour (~$230/month) Network ingress is free, but egress to the public internet is billed at $0.08 per GB. Data transfer within the same CloudScale region is free of charge.

question: How do I add new team members to my organization?
expected_answer: Navigate to the Console, select Settings -> Members tab, and click Invite Member. Enter their email address, choose a role (Owner, Admin, Developer, or Viewer), and click Send Invitation. The invite email link is valid for 7 days.
reference_context: To collaborate with others, you can invite them to your CloudScale organization: 1. Navigate to the Console and select Settings from the sidebar. 2. Click on the Members tab. 3. Click the Invite Member button in the top right. 4. Enter the email address of the team member. 5. Select a Role: Owner, Admin, Developer, or Viewer. 6. Click Send Invitation. The invitee will receive an email link valid for 7 days.

question: What roles are available for team members and what can they do?
expected_answer: The available roles are: Owner (full access, billing, and deletion), Admin (manages resources, settings, and invites; no billing or deletion), Developer (manages apps, databases, and services; no settings or roles), and Viewer (read-only access to services and logs).
reference_context: 5. Select a Role: - Owner: Full access, including billing and organization deletion. - Admin: Can manage all resources, settings, and invites, but cannot view billing or delete the organization. - Developer: Can manage applications, databases, and services, but cannot edit organization settings or member roles. - Viewer: Read-only access to all services and logs.

question: How does auto-scaling work for compute instances?
expected_answer: Auto-scaling is enabled under the Compute Group's Scaling tab. You define the minimum and maximum instances, choose a trigger metric (like CPU Utilization > 70% sustained for 3 minutes), and configure a cooldown period (default 300 seconds). Scaling checks occur every 60 seconds, and provisioning takes 15-30 seconds.
reference_context: Yes, CloudScale supports auto-scaling for Compute Groups. You can define auto-scaling policies based on CPU utilization, memory usage, or HTTP request rates: 1. Go to the Compute section in the console. 2. Select your Compute Group. 3. Click the Scaling tab. 4. Toggle Auto-scaling to ON. 5. Set the Minimum Instances (default: 1) and Maximum Instances (default: 10). 6. Choose the Trigger Metric (e.g., CPU Utilization > 70% sustained for 3 minutes). 7. Configure the Cooldown Period (default: 300 seconds) to prevent rapid scaling loops. Auto-scaling checks occur every 60 seconds. New instances are provisioned in approximately 15-30 seconds.

question: What are the limitations of static website hosting?
expected_answer: Static sites have a maximum file size limit of 100MB per single asset. If a static site exceeds 10GB in total size, it will be throttled or require an upgrade to the Enterprise plan.
reference_context: Can I host static websites on CloudScale? Yes, CloudScale Static Sites provides optimized, CDN-backed hosting for static assets (HTML, CSS, JS, images). ... Limits: The maximum file size for a single asset uploaded to a Static Site is 100MB. Sites exceeding 10GB of total size will be throttled or prompt an upgrade to the Enterprise plan.

question: How is SSL configured for custom domains?
expected_answer: SSL/TLS certificates are automatically provisioned and renewed via Let's Encrypt. Add your domain in domains settings, point your CNAME record to the CloudScale endpoint (or ALIAS/ANAME for root domains), and once DNS propagates, verification and certificate issuance take place within 10 minutes.
reference_context: CloudScale automatically provisions and renews SSL/TLS certificates via Let's Encrypt for all custom domains attached to Compute Groups and Static Sites. 1. Add your custom domain in the Domains settings page of your service. 2. Point your domain's DNS CNAME record to your CloudScale app endpoint (e.g., app-123.cloudscale.net). If configuring a root domain, use an ALIAS or ANAME record, or configure DNS mapping through CloudScale's DNS servers. 3. Once the DNS propagates, CloudScale will verify ownership and issue the certificate within 10 minutes. 4. HTTP traffic is redirected to HTTPS automatically.

question: What is the SLA uptime guarantee for PostgreSQL databases?
expected_answer: The Development Tier (single node) has a 99.9% uptime SLA and lacks automatic failover. The Production Tier (multi-AZ) has a 99.99% uptime SLA and features synchronous replication to a standby node, failing over automatically in under 30 seconds if the primary node fails.
reference_context: We offer two tiers of SLA for Managed PostgreSQL: - Development Tier (Single Node): 99.9% uptime SLA. Suitable for development and staging, but not recommended for production because it lacks automated failover. - Production Tier (High Availability / Multi-AZ): 99.99% uptime SLA. Features synchronous replication to a standby instance in a separate Availability Zone. In the event of a primary node failure, failover occurs automatically within 30 seconds with zero data loss.

question: What happens if CloudScale fails to meet its PostgreSQL uptime SLA?
expected_answer: You are eligible for service credits on your monthly billing: 10% credit if monthly uptime is below 99.9%, and 25% credit if uptime drops below 99.0%.
reference_context: If we fail to meet these uptime guarantees in a given billing month, you are eligible for service credits (10% credit for uptime < 99.9%, 25% credit for uptime < 99.0%).

question: How long are backups of deleted databases retained?
expected_answer: Backups of deleted databases (a Final Backup) are retained for 30 days, unless you chose 'Purge Immediately' during the deletion process.
reference_context: How do I recover a deleted database? When a managed database (Postgres or Redis) is deleted, CloudScale retains a Final Backup for 30 days unless "Purge Immediately" was explicitly selected during deletion.

question: What is the process to restore a deleted database?
expected_answer: Go to Databases -> Backups tab -> Filter by Deleted Databases. Find your database, click Restore, specify a new name and compute plan, and click Confirm. Restoring takes 5 to 15 minutes depending on the data size.
reference_context: To restore a deleted database: 1. Navigate to the Databases dashboard. 2. Select the Backups tab from the top navigation. 3. Filter by Deleted Databases. 4. Locate the database in the list and click Restore. 5. Choose a new name for the restored database instance and select the compute plan. 6. Click Confirm Restore. The restore process will spin up a new database instance and load the backup, which typically takes between 5 and 15 minutes depending on the dataset size.

question: How do I connect to a Postgres database from my local machine?
expected_answer: Add your local IP to the Whitelisted IPs in the Database settings -> Network & Security tab. Download the CloudScale Root CA certificate and use it with your connection string with `sslmode=require` or `sslmode=verify-ca`. The connection string format is `postgresql://[user]:[password]@[host]:5432/[db_name]?sslmode=require`.
reference_context: For security, Managed Postgres databases do not expose public endpoints by default. To connect from your local machine: 1. IP Whitelisting: Go to your Database settings -> Network & Security tab. Add your local IP address to the Whitelisted IPs list. 2. SSL Connection: CloudScale requires SSL connections. Download the CloudScale Root CA certificate from the console and use the sslmode=verify-ca or sslmode=require flags in your connection string. 3. Connection String Format: postgresql://[user]:[password]@[host]:5432/[db_name]?sslmode=require

question: What is the recommended secure connection method for production Postgres databases?
expected_answer: The recommended method for production environments is to configure a secure SSH Tunnel through a bastion host located inside your CloudScale VPC.
reference_context: Alternatively, you can configure a secure SSH Tunnel through a bastion host inside your CloudScale VPC, which is the recommended method for production environments.

question: Does Managed Postgres support read-only replicas in different regions?
expected_answer: Yes, Managed Postgres supports up to 5 read-only cross-region replicas. Replication is asynchronous with replica lag usually under 1 second. Replicas can be promoted to standalone primaries in a disaster. Egress replication traffic is billed at $0.08/GB.
reference_context: Does CloudScale support multi-region replication for databases? Yes, Managed Postgres supports read-only cross-region replicas: - You can create up to 5 read replicas in any available CloudScale region. - Replication is asynchronous, and typical cross-region replica lag is under 1 second under normal workloads. - Replicas can be promoted to a standalone primary database in the event of a disaster or region outage. - Cross-region replication incurs standard network egress charges ($0.08/GB) for all replicated write traffic.

question: Why is my build failing with an out of memory error during deployment?
expected_answer: By default, build containers are limited to 2GB of RAM. If your build script exceeds this, it fails with an OOM. You can fix this by optimizing dependencies, setting NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=1536" for Node.js, or upgrading your build plan for 4GB or 8GB RAM build containers.
reference_context: Why is my build failing with "out of memory" error? An "out of memory" (OOM) error during the build phase typically means your application's build script exceeded the RAM limit of the build container. By default, CloudScale build containers are allocated 2GB of RAM. To fix this: 1. Optimize dependencies. 2. Node.js Memory Limit: Set NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=1536". 3. Upgrade Build Plan: Upgrade to Pro (4GB RAM) or Max (8GB RAM) containers.

question: What are the specs and costs for upgraded build containers?
expected_answer: CloudScale offers two upgraded build container tiers: Pro Build Containers (4GB RAM) for an extra flat fee of $5/month, and Max Build Containers (8GB RAM) for an extra flat fee of $15/month.
reference_context: We offer Pro Build Containers (4GB RAM) and Max Build Containers (8GB RAM) for an additional flat fee of $5/month and $15/month respectively.

question: What managed backup options are provided for databases?
expected_answer: CloudScale offers automated daily backups retained for 7 days (standard) or 30 days (production plans). Production databases also support Point-in-Time Recovery (PITR) to any second in the last 14 days. You can also take manual snapshots which are kept indefinitely, stored in geo-replicated object storage.
reference_context: Does CloudScale offer managed backup services? Yes, CloudScale provides automated daily backups for all managed database instances. - Retention: Daily backups are retained for 7 days on the standard plan, and up to 30 days on the production database plans. - Point-in-Time Recovery (PITR): Production Tier databases support PITR, allowing you to restore your database to any specific second within the last 14 days. - Manual Snapshots: You can trigger a manual backup snapshot at any time. Manual backups are retained indefinitely. - Storage: Backups are stored in geo-replicated object storage and do not consume your database's allocated disk space.

question: Can I host a Next.js or Hugo static site on CloudScale?
expected_answer: Yes, CloudScale Static Sites supports frameworks like Hugo, Gatsby, Next.js (SSG), Vite, and Jekyll. It automatically builds and deploys your site on every push to your connected GitHub repository.
reference_context: Can I host static websites on CloudScale? Yes, CloudScale Static Sites provides optimized, CDN-backed hosting for static assets (HTML, CSS, JS, images). - Deployment: Connect your GitHub repository, and CloudScale will automatically build and deploy your site on every push. Supported frameworks include Hugo, Gatsby, Next.js (SSG), Vite, and Jekyll.

question: Is data transfer free within the same CloudScale region?
expected_answer: Yes, network data transfer within the same CloudScale region is free of charge.
reference_context: Data transfer within the same CloudScale region is free of charge.

question: How long is the invitation sent to team members valid?
expected_answer: The invitation link sent to a team member is valid for 7 days.
reference_context: 6. Click Send Invitation. The invitee will receive an email link valid for 7 days.
